channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 237 replies — 948.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 237 • Updated: 2026-07-09 23:55 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62464263
>>62464047
Who the fuck cares? The fact that all of this bothers you more then having to wait and d word niggers having to perform in both threads is incredibly telling. Do a flip nigger.

62464317
>>62464263
That based alpha CHAD who posts about the Cusip Conundrum does so out of excitement because his investment could pay off handsomely. Who sits around seethe posting about a stock they don't own. Even if you didn't invest in BBBYQ, all GME shareholders would want to see RC's machincations come to fruititon. A loss for shorts on BBBYQ would be a good thing for GME shareholders as that would undermine the margin collateral propping up the shorts. But these silly shillies don't seem to care at all about this.
Curious.

62464342
>>62464263
>who cares about AlexSneed
>you have to be bothered by what im bothered by
Fuck you alex you dumb nigger. Youre going to prison along with Travis, ABC and the other insider wallets

62464714
>>62464342
RICO ROUNDUPPPPPOOO

62464716
>>62464052
>>62464263
I want to get paid for my investment and don't care about any of these people. But it would be a bonus if PP got prosecuted for financial crime.

62464724
>>62464716
There won't be any political capital to go after someone like PP when the short hedgie crimes are piling up and causing some very serious issues for the U.S. capital markets. They'll want to pay us and sweep this under the rug. Prosecuting PP would not go over well when his audience, now millionaires, start making a stink.

62464731
>>62464724
I have a hard time believing anybody gives a shit about that guy

62464732
>>62437938

62464772
>>62464731
When we find out that we won the PP show is going to be the place to be. People may be sick of the grifting and the baseless and endless hype posts he makes but once it finally happens even the haters will be watching.

62464881
>>62441565
>Amelie Warren
that's some terrible taste lmao

62464891
>>62464772
The haters are already watching Kek. They can’t stop talking about his wife in both threads and they’re literally reading court filings for his divorce.

62464895
>>62464891
They are convinced that Sonichu is both PP and ABC in disguise.

62464897
>>62464895
PP HAS to be every towel knower.

62464909
>>62464895
>everyone is PP
I am not PP, debunked. Shills can not dispute the holiday inn dilemma

62464919
>>62464772
$GABBBYQ has already won, this is why she hasn’t been since the 18th

62464943
I’m starting to think this is a larp

62464998
>>62464943
>The CUSIP Conundrum, the Director Dilemma, and the Affable Affiliates are all an elaborate ruse.

62465011
>>62464998
>The Gabbbyq Gape at the Holiday Inn
Interesting

62465133
>>62464943
What does live action role playing have to do with the bankruptcy of bed bath and beyond?

62465151
>>62464047
that's the thing about starting a religion. You have to figure out how to monetize it

so he creates a proxy for BBBY and then pretends it's being crucified by the romans just like your messiah was. Then he can trick some of you fools into buying it before rugging you like the dumbass sheep you are.

>>62464998
those things are obvious schizophrenic apophenia. What's not clear is if your schizophrenia is real or affected.

62465330
The shills were instructed to spam shit about the personal life of theppshow host because pulte(director of national intelligence) frequenting it and Larry cheng going on after shares were cancelled, were huge signals that told retail bbbyq is coming back.

Even more important is that the cusip-6 of bbbyq and the bbby warrants match. Shillies stopped having their mods frantically delete posts last fall/winter and now they just spam and flood to try and avoid the curiosity altogether.

62465397
>>62465330
Ok debunk the divorce filings in Utah and why Mrs PP has not been seen since the 18th and Ryan Cohen tweeted a T Rex image in the Bahamas

You can’t shilly willy

62465412
all they have left is forced satire and inauthentic contrived meme energy that never sticks which is why the AMC spam (attempt to discredit the CUSIP-6) stopped because this stuff never works and people see right through it.

62465418
>>62465412
silo the topic as much as you want, you can’t refute the holiday inn

62465422
>>62465418
I guess I'll take the bait.

You think focusing on the shows host is more important that the content discussed on the show? Or prominent figures who went onto the show who are very close to RC? Of course you don't because you're paid to be here.

62465431
>>62465422
I fully agree with you, Ryan has been inside the PP suite at the holiday inn as well, we all have. We are all waiting 9 months now for the divorce to conclude and we can all celebrate, at the holiday inn.

62465456
You'd think the shill sissies would be uploading these divorce documents or whatever the fuck they're talking about since they already uploaded PP's resume and everything else. Makes you wonder if they're just...making things up with zero basis whatsoever. Hard to blame them cause these boomers and jeets really can't meme. Between dougshit, CUSIP Conundrum, etc. they really have no way of controlling sentiment so they just make the lamest copies trying to capture any bit of meme energy.

62465498
>>62465456
Do your own research, the cuck chair rarely comes pre heated, but when it does, PP is there.

Funny how you haven’t disproven the (1) thing yet you refuse discuss, interesting

62465544
>>62465456

item.title: House buying — 104 replies — 416.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
House buying

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-07-09 23:57 UTC

OP

I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.

What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.

Early replies (first 3)

62447251
>>62447246
>every normie I meet says
You could buy and then the market severely crashes.
Housing shouldn't be a financial asset but that's corporatism for you.

62447257
>>62447251
Yea i guess but the stock market "could" also crash and i lose everything. That's not really insightful advice.

62447265
Lmao you are a rentoid

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450606
>>62448058
Jealous blk boi

62450608
>>62450600
Rentoids all suck at math their anecdotes are fake as fuck but real in their bitter minds

62450897
My house is paid off. Feels great man. 6 bedrooms 3.5 baths on half an acre. Great home for my family of 6.

62451180
>>62450600
You can't even follow basic math. He had a $190k loan at around 6%. Hardly $500 a month with insurance, taxes, mortgage insurance, etc.

>>62450608
My cost to rent is 11% of my take home pay. You are spending more on a mortgage with no guarantee of keeping your home.

Biz really has the dumbest anons.

62454567
>>62447246
I unironically read this book

62454883
>>62450897
Sounds shitty man, i can just save and invest without being bled dry by a bunch of kids who are gonna grow up trans

62455019
>>62447246
When you buy a house, if you do, if you're going to rent it out, make sure you put a large enough down payment to make it cash flow $600+/mo.
Basically getting your money back little by little
and that 600 surplus will cover expenses as they come up, and someone else pays the equity for you, and you get the interest & depreciation deductions.
One big mistake with my rentals is putting the minimum down.
Expenses come out of my pocket mostly.
Remember taxes go up, insurance goes up, rezoned to a FEMA flood zone to lay for coastal homes, HOA goes up, everything goes up, so even if you increase rent, you're not taking it home, because you're paying those increased costs which go up every year.
You can make money in RE if you can fix the places yourself. If you're not handy? Think about something else.
>t. not handy and it costs me an arm and a leg and construction contractors are the worst, even the "premium" ones can't install a level floor.

62456167
>>62447246
Houses are for gays. Real chads life onnastreets with a dividend portfolio.

62456179
When the cashier at 7-11 gives housing advice the market is going to fall out.

62456187
>>62448648
>That'll be 50k to hook up your house to utilities and its mandatory in this county otherwise we condemn your building.

62456204
180k isn't enough
you need to make 100k a year to enjoy owning a home having a family etc

62456307
>>62447246
Rentoids will spend 30 years paying off someone else's house just to tell you you're bad at investing.
Bull owns the land.
Cuck pays the bull every month.
If you don't own land by retirement, you got outplayed.

62458331
I gave up on getting a house. I want to live in a cheap RV but even RV park rent here is over $1 a month.

62458370
>>62458331
I looked into this clear back in the 1990's. I figured just buy some cheap land and park a mobile home on it. I live in a rural area that used to be super libertarian anti-government so I thought I could do it.

it wasn't legal even back then. I had to park in a trailer park and pay lot rent which was just as much as renting an apt. The rules are stupid, they don't want people living in an affordable manner. They want you buying houses and propping up the prices.

62458377
>>62456187
White incels have endless excuses why they are losers

62458381
>>62458370
>>62458370
No moron they don't want you shitting in your backyard and being a nuisance . You are stupid not the rules

62458389
>>62458381
how's your attempts to improve board traffic with rage bait going?

as far as I can tell it's actually getting worse the more you post.

62458912
>>62456307
Calm your clitty down, this isn't an erotic roleplaying thread. I plan to buy a house some day but not NOW as a single incel in the prime of my life. I'll buy it when I'm wealthy.

62459068
>>62450897
>half an acre
>good for family
wew lad

62459305
>>62458389
He's angry because he's bald

62459602
>>62447271
>Mortgageoids always make it seem like some magical thing where you flip the house for $100k any time you want
They just can't do math, seriously. Your dipshit coworker will burn 100k on maintenance/interest, sell for 60k more than they bought, and brag about making money. They honestly just don't account for the costs along the way.

62460735
>>62447246
I read these forum posts and did the numbers myself, buying a house is retarded right now with interest rates as high as they are and rent is cheap as it is. I just see leagues of fucking boomers/Gen Xers mad they can't find a sucker to buy their dump for 2022 prices no inspections and real estate agents malding that they can't jew out 15k closing costs for doing almost nothing.

62462019
>>62447246
$180k isn't buying you a house. Even if you got something cheap ($350k), the PMI alone with that down payment is around $1,600, which is higher than your rent. Internet, utilities, and trash are going to be another couple hundred. You also need to consider inspections, closing costs, and immediate repairs you want to make too.

62462025
>>62459602
>They honestly just don't account for the costs along the way
we do since we write that stuff off on our taxes

you pay that stuff too, but since it's included in your rent you're not aware of it. In fact you pay everything we do PLUS profits for your landlord.

62462134
>>62447246
>you're making a mistake not getting a 7% mortgage goyim!

62462612
>>62462025
"We" is not the normgroid I'm talking about, try not to be so defensive.

62462632
>>62447271

owning a house can be expensive. i can't even begin to list all the things i've spent money that I never even considered. i would suggest saving substantially more than whatever you intend to make as a down payment. we did 20% and had another $150k put aside for 1st year stuff. we didn't spend it all, but probably half and could've easily spent more. "saving money" is a lost concept. right now it's just treading water. hopefully, in a few years things will improve - but I'm glad we were in a good place before we bought.

the good part is, it's your house.

62462637
>>62447246
Don’t listen to retards that don’t know jack shit about the market keep dollar cost averaging in the index

62465502
>>62447251

62465549
>>62447246
Housing is a pyramid scheme. Once the boomers are dead it will probably get cheaper, although real demand is in cities and that probably won't disappear.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 67 replies — 268.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-07-10 00:00 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>Good /biz/ manga
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Good /biz/ movies
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Born_Rich_(2003_film)

>Good /biz/ books
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gambler_(novel)

>Good /biz/ games
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Good /biz/ waifus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Holmes

>Agenda 2030 is on the way, have you studied what your enemy has planned out?
https://www.math.uwaterloo.ca/~ervrscay/TheGreatReset.pdf

Praeviously
>>62373752

Early replies (first 3)

62453729
All you faggots need to do is buy chainlink. Fuck your side hustles. You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.

62453795
Last thread we spoke a lot on learning new skills but very few fruitful things arose. I realized the issue is that a lot of the proposed skills were just boring or too niche.

62453799
>>62453795
wait, are you asking poor people to discuss amongst themselves how to get rich?
kek

you don't learn how to get rich from poor people

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62458473
>>62458425
i know the rise and fall is becoming less and less, but a lot of people are scared to invest in it is my thoughts and its always 'the last bull cycle with big gains"

62458727
>>62458289
doing WHAT?

62458801
>>62453805
That's only $10k...

62458804
>>62453846
Poor.

62459556
>>62453729
>You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.
For 7k now.
:)

62461612
Bump

62461928
Off work, now to devote my energies to this general

62461982
>>62457877
>>62457792
What sells a good memecoin is not only the narrative but the art accompanied alongside it. In this case, we know that

>Animals

Are positively received.

I cannot think of any legendary memecoin runner(multi-billion) that had a human at its front and center. It's usually animals, and commonly known animals like pets or frogs.

I think for this thread as we do our research on art we can also practice drawing as a way to become more valuable and ultimately increase our net worth.

62462009
Who here could obtain this job? All of you could, it will just come down to whether the individual hiring believes you have the qualifications.

Let's take a further examination into a high paying role such as this. Let's see what sort of requirements are needed to make $50 an hour.

62462035
you ever had a job that scaled like this?

62462047
Is this beyond your capabilities?

62462063
Is all your money in dollars?

Are you not aware that art itself can also be a place to store value in?

With a resurgence in European culture spreading out through the world, with the increase of non white races, won't people desire a form of art that was once dominant in the world?

ESPECIALLY as we get more and more digital?

62462167
>>62462035

>Question
>what makes art sell, what similarities did the memecoins ( >>62457792 ) &( >>62457877 )

Answer:
they were dogs

>Question
>what do we have to learn, make a high amount of money in a short amount of time?

Answer is right here --> >>62462047

Question
>What forms of art can we store money in

????

62462792
Have to go to bed,

Goodnight and bump

62462799
>>62457657
Must you broadcast your ethnic insecurity all over 4channel.org(slash) biz?

62462804
>>62454046
>mom look I posted it again!

62462813
I finally paid off my high Apr CC debt so now I can stack paychecks for a while. I’ve been good about cutting down spending in general. I wonder if I should cut out meat and just do rice and beans? Can you get similar nutrition from beans? Things are certainly not looking great because I care about my job less by the day but at least I paid off debt.

I don’t wanna /blog too hard but fuck life is sucking lately, I just hope it’s better in a year. I been working nonstop for two years haven’t had any pto either

62462824
>>62453622
>yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen
go work literally any job for like 6 months

62462992
>UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

OH NO NO NO

AHAHAHAHAHAHA

LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

62464145
>>62462813
Rent truly is the worst bloodsucker. What are you saving for? Just to have reserve funds? You were spending all your excess money on debt and not saving/investing? Genuinely asking

62464163
>>62462813
Also I would be careful with poor nutrition depending on your age. If under 25 then take the risk with cheap foods.

62465232
Bump, almost done waging

62465510
alright today, lets practice looking at good designs of top runners and seeing if we can emulate the artists ability. Let's also look at great /biz/ memes and ask ourselves, ""what made this meme so popular" like it was an actual gallery

62465526
Who is this? What makes him so special? Why is he one of the most identifiable mascots on this site?

62465529
Forgot the image, what about this characters DESIGN made him so well loved

62465547
this is what knowyourmeme has to say on Mumu

side note: alt girls get paid to make videos like this? what a job!

62465548
what will happen when we reach #10k thread?

62465553
the absolute state of this board

62465556
>>62453622
Quads and I’ll chop my cock off

62465561
>>62465556
dude...

item.title: /biz/ 8 years ago >BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X! — 37 replies — 39.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
/biz/ 8 years ago >BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X!

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-07-09 23:13 UTC

OP

/biz/ 8 years ago
>BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X! YOU JUST NEED 1K DOLLARS
/biz/ 5 years ago
>OMG BRO SOMEONE THREW 3500 BUCKS/10 ETH INTO THIS COIN, WE HAVE WHALES! I'M UP 400K TODAY!
/biz/ now
>You should NEVER do calls, options, invest in any kind of crypto no matter the bull case, and if a stock is going up a lot you should be afraid because it will crash no matter what. Instead you should start a business- a real one, like contracting or landscaping, not an ecommerce business- and put your spare paychecks into VOO for 40 years.

Where did these people come from? The board is fucking dead but did we get an influx of unironic retired boomers giving out advice that 'worked' in 1966 and is now totally fucking worthless?

Early replies (first 3)

62453596
>>62453585
we've always had 3 or 4 boomers here. But now everyone else left.

coincidentally all those other "people" left when they made the capcha harder and made ban evasion more difficult.

62453601
>>62453585
Jannies killed the board. You cant share alpha or gambles or anything here now.

62453607
cant share any cryptos that arent in the top 100 coins lmao. the 2024/2025 janny (if theyre still here) turned this place into bag holder general

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453878
>>62453585
Circumstances have changed, I’m unironically smiling at my VOO and AIQ gains yet back then I was buying options and cryptos. All that money is gone now. I’m 30 now I need to think about my future seriously.
>>62453842
Based. We are expecting the 50% pullback and I will hold it like Charlie Munger says you ought to.

62453881
>>62453585
Its a glow psyop now.

62454979
>>62453585
maybe people learned not to waste money snd time on dangerous "investments"

62455028
>>62453878
>Based. We are expecting the 50% pullback and I will hold it like Charlie Munger says you ought to.
Like a billionaire who insider traded the market and lived in luxury until he died at almost 100 told you to?

62455042
>>62453601
>>62453607
This.
Try posting something that isnt a normie popular investment and it gets nuked instantly.

62455297
Nice thumbnail bro.

62455311
>>62453585
/biz/ now is more
>Start a power washing business and buy tech stocks

62460876
>>62460845
That's another gay bagholder meme that gets spammed on this board.

62461091
>>62460876
ur a gay bagholder meme

62461128
>>62453585
i just want to say i hate kikes, niggers, chinks, trannies, jeets and women
that is all

62461155
>>62461128
Why is bobo drinking piss?

62462421
>>62453585
>>You should NEVER do calls, options, invest in any kind of crypto no matter the bull case, and if a stock is going up a lot you should be afraid because it will crash no matter what. Instead you should start a business- a real one, like contracting or landscaping, not an ecommerce business- and put your spare paychecks into VOO for 40 years.

>Where did these people come from?

/biz/ had these guys in 2016 too lol, we've gone full circle

62464241
>>62453585
it really is that easy.

62464311
>>62453585
Every crypto bullrun sees a new batch of intelligent anons make it and leave, and a new batch of retarded anons arrive. It's a grim downward spiral

62464368
>>62453607
I quit coming here in 2022 partially because of this was most threads. Why didn't keep a shit coins general like with PMs? Oh that is right Indians can't stop spamming meme coins in their own threads.

62464378
>>62453585
>you should start a business- a real one, like contracting or landscaping, not an ecommerce business- and put your spare paychecks into VOO for 40 years.
You should.
>>62453607
Because all jannies are xrp, link and monero bagholders.

62464445
>>62453601
this

if i posted $cashcat here when i bought at 3m i would have been banned, and it ran to 140m a few days later

why arent we allowed to have even 1 memecoin general up? those get banned too

62464533
>>62453596
Sus

62464549
>>62453585
>You should NEVER do options
True
>invest in any kind of crypto no matter the bull case
Nobody says this.
>if a stock is going up a lot you should be afraid
True
>.... because it will crash no matter what
Not necessarily
>Instead you should start a business- a real one, like contracting or landscaping, not an ecommerce business- and put your spare paychecks into VOO for 40 years.
Nobody says this.

62464589
I guess they all went to wsb like the redditors they always were. You should join them

62464590
>>62453585
Yes because 8 years ago crypto still had room to run, it was also still exciting and people genuinely believed in it. I wouldn’t say crypto is totally dead but it’s changed, we saw some crazy runs and now it’s cooling off. The 4 year cycle also seems like it’s dead.

62464740
>>62464590
>I wouldn’t say crypto is totally dead
I’ll say it. It’s fucking dead. I would even go as far as to say it died in 2024, despite that mini alt run that happened. Coinbase couldn’t onboard 1b people. I don’t think they even onboarded 1k people desu. No one cares anymore. ETH/BTC prices are irrelevant to me because that shit is fake along with the volume. I wish it wasn’t like this because crypto was the only thing that gave me hope in making it, but it is what it is. No point trying to cope

62464750
>>62453585
I've been here since the board was created and I have always been against crypto. /biz/ is not only about fucking crypto you zoomer trash.

62464777
>>62464750
But it’s the zoomers who dgaf about crypto

62464790
>>62464777
Millennials are rock hoarders or stocks fags. Zoomers are cryptoshills and got liquidated by believing jeet spam. If anything, there's more boomers that shill crypto than there are millennials. I saw a 60+ year old boomer wearing an XRP hat at an outlet store the other day.

62464812
>>62464750
/biz/ was created to contain bitcoin threads though.

62464836
>>62464750
>BREAKING: Old man screams at the wind
>more at 7

62465128
>>62464790
For me, it’s millennials, then boomers. The millennials were shilling that shit hard and all the diehard TAtard crypto analysts that I’ve seen over the years have been millennials. Most of the shitfluencers were as well. Zoomers were supposed to be the exit liquidity but the jeets and jews got too carried away this cycle, and sol drove the nail in the coffin

62465465
>>62455297
Poor Elephant.

item.title: Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed. — 38 replies — 34.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-07-09 23:03 UTC

OP

Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Now many crypto's like XRP and Chainlink are no longer illegal and the banks can start buying it.

Are you ready for the pump of a lifetime in the coming weeks?

Early replies (first 3)

62444894
I just took 70mg of illicit amphetamine sulfate

62444895
>>62444894
That's very nice to hear, I hope you are enjoying yourself.

62444904
>>62444895
it's nothing like meth (oral ROA meth had no euphoria either), minimal vasoconstriction; none really. not euphoric like adderall either, it feels like super non-diuretic caffeine. this has a 1:1 dextro:racemic ratio; so the pharma equivalent is evekeo.

I thought it was bunk but my heart rate is elevated but no arrhythmia like meth (that shit is poison, it'll keep you up for 3-5 days with no perceptual fatigue at significant cost)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62444939
>>62444934

I actually dont know much about that also

62444940
>>62444939
you sound like a nice guy no homo

62444948
these 2 got married y'know

62444959
>>62444940
Haha thanks man you too

>>62444948
Nooo we are just friends haha

62444978
>>62444892
> CLARITY ACT
wasn't there some announcement announced for yesterday?

62444991
op they haven't voted on it and aren't close to voting on it. they're hoping to get it done before august 10

62444995
>>62444991
Somone told me clarity act was 4th of july a few weeks ago

62445015
>>62444948
naw, the no homo made marriage impossible
>>62444959
>we are just friends
this

62445147
>>62444978
No, Trump did an interview restating that the US will be the crypto capital of the world.
>>62444892
CLARITY will pass, but there is a lot going behind the scenes that's causing it to stall. Did you know that CLARITY allows banks to buy and sell crypto? The last time US banks traded stocks was before the great depression.

62445160
>>62445147
Oh that's very interesting, thanks for sharing my friend

62445194
So, two more weeks?

62445212
>>62444948
you're responding to a samefag trying to keep his thread at the top because no one cares.

>>62444892
no one cares

62445217
>>62445212
that's not me

62445568
Why isnt crypto pumping harder?

62448360
>>62444892
The banks can already buy both. ETF inflows broke records despite being from small issuers on XRP, highest of any asset on the year, while inflows are borderline non-existent for link.

62448734
Everyone itt thread is a retarded bot.
Fake thread.

62450765
>>62444892
BOOOOOOOOOM

62450968
I hate niggers

62451473
>>62450968
Kaboom

62454381
>>62444892
>banks start buying it
okay jeet

62454867
>XRP

So. Fucking. Bullish.

62458344
>>62444892
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62458384
>>62444892
>this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.
nope

62458627
>>62444995
that date was always nonsense because they are literally in recess from the end of june until july 13. they will have another month to pass it before they go into recess yet again in august

62459378
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62460039
>>62444978
2 more weeks turned into 1 more month

62461072
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62461114
>>62460039
It's not going to pass.

Republicans don't have enough votes to force it through congress and in a few months republicans are getting kicked out of congress.

62465432
>>62444904
Cathinones > meth

62465442
>>62461114
that doesnt mean anything
democrats could easily vote for it

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-09 23:58 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463754
It’s still on coingecko so you need to learn how to use google search correctly my friend

62464735
>>62461988
nobody is buying your bags bro and this MICA shit will not pump your very heavy bags lmaoooo

62465284
>>62461988
The flaw tih Everest is that you're trying to pump a project that supports regulations preventing P&D, with a "utility" token that has zero use outside being used as gas in beta test by one or two b2b customer, no fabled VNO until now as well, so where's the utility?

It is clear that the MICA application was not because it is mandatory, but it is also a last ditch attempt to make themselves more attractive to raise external funds, because there's no way they could obtain meaningful capital by selling more token at this price, so they get MICA to pitch it to institutional while it still warm; before inevitably many other similar project also obtain MICA and overtake Everest. Reminder that Everest has lost grounds on KYC, fiat on ramp, RWA products even to companies that don't use proprietary token.

62465296
>got it before Binance

oof

62465554
>>62465284
>It is clear that the MICA application was not because it is mandatory
Yeah anything you say can’t be taken seriously if you typed that shit

item.title: Why aren’t more anons cops? — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren’t more anons cops?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-10 00:07 UTC

OP

You can abuse and arrest non whites, and if they shoot you and kill you then you successfully KYS’d without needed to pull the trigger yourself. It’s literally killing two birds with one stone potentially.

Early replies (first 3)

62465528
>>62465494
I was thinking about it right around the end of high school and I came to the conclusion that I'd rather join the military if I had to. For reference, I'm a Euro (non-western). In the end I just finished a tech uni and got an office job.

62465552
>>62465528
I didn’t even know there were euro police officers. I thought the entire point of Europe was to get raped in the ass by Arabs and Africans with HIV. Pepe’s are obviously American cops in the meme. So fuck off.

62465568
I did it. Quit after 5 years. It’s full of NPC’s, politics, lesbians, backstabbers, militaryfags, and buying into the “thin blue line” propaganda. You will only work with cops, date other cops, fuck other cops, get divorced and become and alcoholic. Life expectancy is 3-5 years after retirement. I was a good officer. I saved someone’s life and didn’t get any recognition because I was fucking the prized female senior officer.

I hate cops more than ever now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62465575
>>62465568
What was she on a scale of 1-10

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-07-09 23:11 UTC

OP

This just got CTO’d. Free money on the ground.

random alt crypto bullshit is dead. Just accept it.

Kys op. Saged

62462471

Dont rug me nigger

62462510
hell yea brother,,,,,this one is the moon we shoot for,,,,,

62462776

Idk why I try. I’ve posted so many 100xs here and you dumb niggers always fade. Just pretend it’s chainlink

62462786

Oh yeah? let’s see this list and archived posts of your multiple 100x’s you dirty brown. I’ll even simplify it for you, show me 2 archived posts of yours. All fields btw

62465449
>>62462776
This aged very poorly incase any anons were wondering

62465460
>>62462358
SARS PLEASE BUY SARS BE RICH AND FUCK MANY WHITE WOMEN SAR BUY BUY

item.title: Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production — 63 replies — 64.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-07-09 23:10 UTC

OP

DTCC is going live on-chain later this year.
Yet crypto is in the shitter, why?

Early replies (first 3)

62463146
>>62463144
Explain how it's bullish for crypto?

62463270
>>62463146
Both Swift and DTCC are going to be using a variety of public chains.

62463273
So SWIFT has their own singular chain. I thought it'd be that each bank has their own chain, connected to each other by CCIP. If it's all done on a single chain then it obviates the need for a lot of CCIP transactions, right?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463904
>>62463883
I'm asking when are they going live bro
>you're pretending not to have "MY chain"
what in the flying fuck are you talking about dude? you're completely deranged

62463912
>>62463883
lurk more, retard

62463914
>>62463904
>I'm asking when are they going live bro
Why are you asking me?
If Swift and those 17 banks are lying, that's on them and not me.

>what in the flying fuck are you talking about dude?
I asked you what "your chain" was, and you implied you didn't have any.

62463917
>>62463901
>>62463912
what are you even sperging about

62463923
>>62463917
just lurk.
stop posting. and lurk.

62463939
>>62463923
>get demolished
>switch IDs and start randomly sperging in non-sequiturs

classic

62463946
legit mental illness, and all this because I dared to question his shilling by simply asking WHEN are those banks going live
dear God
>my ID
>muh YOUR CHAIN
go back to the asylum you unhinged schizoid

62463963
>>62463946
Why are you asking anons for the exact time each of those 17 banks makes their first transaction on this new Swift ledger?
Clearly this is upsetting you greatly.

62463968
Whats up biepum?
Hows that flippening coming along?
Boxing with shadows again I see?
Touch grass baggie

62463973
>>62463968
ID-that-spews-random-butthurt-non-sequiturs number 4

62463981
>>62463939
>multiple people want me to stop posting because all i do is fall for scams and generally make the board a worse place
>tHeY'rE sWiTcHiNg IdS

62463996
>>62463981
>multiple people

62464019
don't bother, the guy is completely unhinged, he's spamming multiple threads doing the same shit, look at this>>62464006

62464021
>>62464019
doing what shit?

62464030
chainlink not used

62464425
fuddies seething

62464488
>>62463146
Chain link is what powers it. Every transaction will have fees paid for in chain link and people will have to stake chain link so that the data nodes are secure. Demand goes up chain link goes $10k each. Study more

62464845
>>62463752
/thread

its funny how the link cult can't understand how the rest of the investment world moved on from the space and woke up years ago about how all of the announcements dont mean anything for altcoins


OP sounds like a boomer complaining no one wants to work anymore

62464853
>>62464488
>duuuuuuuude just research moar buzzzzzwordssss!

yeah hows that going for ya kid

62465008
>>62463808
kek its always funny reading all the mind broken crippletards and the fudkeks here. like
>NONONO YOU CANT MEAN THAT IT IS ALL FAKE YOU MUST WORK WITH XRP OTHERWISE WHAT HAVE I WASTED ALL THESE YEARS FOR

62465020
>>62464853
how's this adem?
https://x.com/The_DTCC/status/2075280707203592232

be honest now, how much did you lose in Wormhole, LayerZero and LinkSol?
imagine sinking your money in literal SCAMS just to stick it to le EBIL linkies and walking away even poorer LMAO

62465065
>>62465020
I hold 1,500 since im a poorfag. Been on this board since 2022 and have seen so many posts about dates and how chainlink will finally rise in price like when they claimed CCIP would make it rise, november of last year because that guy from swift says that they are done piloting, sergey going to the fucking white house and now the clarity act. I dont believe anything until it actually happens, until then ill just keep buying when i can.

62465075
>>62463686
/Biz/ would be fun for a few days

62465077
>>62463552
Now that's what I'm talking about

62465091
>>62463144
>Agentic commerce

Either these buzzwords die or I become very wealthy, winwin

62465124
>>62463144
TOKEN NOT NEEDED

62465212
>>62463146
It’s bullish for chainlink, which is all that matters

62465220
>>62465212
nothing is bullish for chainlink sweetie
it is an eternal dump coin of dumpage

62465269
tick tock fuddies

62465459
>>62463144

everything to do with chainlink

item.title: VOO and Chill — 205 replies — 46.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 205 • Updated: 2026-07-09 19:44 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62436538
>>62436510
Do you think DRAM has value, honest question, and for how long - if so, what dip should one wait for
>>62436499
VGT and QQQ are only doing better thanks to tech having such strong performances for years now, correct?
VOO seems safe since you don't know the future, but on the other hand, thinking the future of the market for the next decades wont always be tech focused seems so short sighted it's silly.....maybe I should start DCA into VGT?

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

62438775
Yep bros
Bought some more VOO and am waiting on my DRAM position to go green so I can sell and not have to deal with thematic ETFs no high expense ratios ever again
Simple as

62440169
>>62431374
>VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses
VOO is passively managed. These 2 girls are not like Kathy Wood picking stocks.

62442752
>>62431374
They could boil alive eat grannies alive for all I care as long as they get the money going, what kind of faggot ass train of thought is this. They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding. "The people making me rich are foids so it makes me icky" is unironically the foidest take I have ever read.
>>62430902
A year ago I started investing for the first time in my life (I'm in my mid 20s) and thought I was very smart and I would just outperform the market 1% profit at a time. After a whole year I only managed to get a net benefit of a 3% after wasting many hours and carrying heavy bags. I learnt my lesson. VOO and chill.

62442988
>>62442752
>They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding.

The portfolio managers of VOO have 0 control over whether VOO compounds or not. VOO is simply an ETF that tracks the S&P 500. Their only job is to make sure VOO follows the S&P 500. Once the fund is set up, there really is not much for them to do. I guess if they haven't automated removing and adding companies to the composition of VOO, then they do that.

62444521
Let’s all say thank you to Jack Bogle

62444531
>>62400515
and yet boomers hold 90% of the wealth and growing...

62445651
>>62421799
>5+ yr 60% drawdown event happens
>buy cheaper voo

62446571
>>62444521
thanks chad bogle

62447653
>>62445651
Kek it really is that simple

62450991
>>62446571
Thanks Chad Bogle!

62454214
>>62413718
Felix Prehn has never been an investment banker. He is an ex artist and wine salesman, now grifter on YouTube who makes money from his "academy" fees (up to 10k for shitty training). He's a regular scammer, like many in this segment.

62455460
It feels good to be a VOO’er

62455475
>>62400493
My retirement funds are $70k and in VOO, anything better to put all that in?

62455990
>>62455475
Look into VPMAX

62456048
>>62455990
Neat

62459187
>>62403053
The median 64 yo is planning to work until they're dead.
Boomers are never going to retire because they can't afford to. They are going to keep working like the good little goyim they are and fall down dead on the job because that's the only way they can fund the monthly payments on all the toys they went into debt to purchase.
I spit on their graves. Fuck boomer scum.

62459238
>>62459187
Lmao, another white incel who knows everything,,,,,what are the odds? 100% at least,,,,,do they use it to get rich? Lmao no

62460695
Yawwwwwn another week another VOO purchase

62460733
>>62460695
i made a whole $5 buying a share of VOO during that dip :) too bad I lost more from all my previous VOO shares

62460835
>>62460733
Sorry to hear that champ maybe DRAM is more your speed

62460844
>>62400995
Lmfao. You do realize that the highest weighted stocks that lead the spy are mostly in QQQ right? Lmfao

62460852
>>62459187
They can afford it

62461238
>>62401051
>I think a decade long CAGR comparison was a 5% difference but I’m not sure if CAGR is the best stat to go by?
It changes significantly depending on when you enter the market. Since 2000 there's only a 0.6% difference in favor of QQQ over SPY, the latter outperformed the former for most of that timeframe. If you bought sometime post-2009, however, you would have been better off holding QQQ by far. The moral of the story is keep an eye on things and only buy more volatile assets if it's a growth period. QQQ will continue to be great if you bought a decade ago until the music stops.

62461412
>>62400515
Fpbp. Bogleheads are the biggest retards on the planet.

62461672
>>62400620
>What makes one rich
Health, family, friends.

62462476
>>62461238
For now I’m still just doing VOO cause it just feels better even if I’m missing out on qqq gains

62464487
It’s payday tomorrow, VOO brahs. There’s nothing risky that looks too appealing so I go all voo maybe some palantir as well. My only regret is taking the work health benefits, my check will be smaller. Two weeks is an eternity to wait for your money.

62464830
Is there any point of voo and chill if I already have a 401k and pension. Know can compound it more but can be more risky right

item.title: 16,000 LINK and Counting — 16 replies — 35.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
16,000 LINK and Counting

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-07-09 23:42 UTC

OP

Niggers, jannies, and fudders TONGUE MY ANUS. Also, is it over for BAT? I still have a pile of it and it's bleeding out faster than Charlie Kirk

Early replies (first 3)

62461808
p+c, stinkie
pee plus heckin' see

62461810
>>62461808
Kek. You again? Bend over and lick my taint, tranny

62461938
>>62461796
damn, you're gonna have 16 million dollars soon. are you at least staking your own 10k? and yeah its absolutely over for BAT, i still have 20k and i will always be pissed it never mooned. i saw a brave ad recently showing people tipping 200-500 BAT, its clear they intend it to stay at .20 cents forever.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62461946
>>62461938
I can't get in the fucking pool since I'm a latefag. But, I don't really care. I'll keep it until if and when they expand it. But yeah. I saw that ad too. Fucking disaster. Roadmap V3 was just a series of bullshit partnerships and a memecoin .Roadmap V4 will make that look good by comparison. Fucking rent seekers

62461966
>>62461946
its been a disaster since 2020 when the entire world needed a safe way to have video calls and brave had it built in, they just never pushed it or advertised it. so many great features of brave go unused because they stopped developing them or they don't tell anyone about them. i'm not even signed up for BAT rewards because i don't want to sign up for uphold. its a big disappointment all around. if i had chosen LINK over BAT from the beginning i'd already be a millionaire. now im only at 4064 link.

62461987
>>62461966
Checked. The sad part is I believe Luke means well. The rest of the team, Jennie and Lobes included, just don't give a fuck. Lobes is holding out for a merger of some sort for sure so that he can have his last boomer pay day. I keep holding hoping for a pump to $1 so I can get a decent 7x on my investment but even that seems impossible now.

62462468
>>62461987
i wouldn't worry too much if i were you, you're about to have 16 million dollars. after taxes probably something like 12 million dollars. you're set for life.

62462785
Uh... thots?

62464025
>>62462468
One can hope.
>>62462785
TP abandoned us bro

62464235
>>62461796
based link holder.

62464341
>>62461946
I don’t really care if latefags make it. I’ve been in since 2017 and shoulda coulda woulda sold a dozen times since then but here I am. If you make it, I make it, and I am content with that. Many lessons learned that will serve me in life as a result of the suffering. Godspeed, marine.

62464514
>>62464025
TP would never ditch, stop the cap

62465339
Roadmap up: https://brave.com/blog/bat-roadmap-4-0/

62465347
>>62465339
>BAT Roadmap
>Year of our Lord 2026
LMAO, might as well start posting about how Bernie can still become president

62465417
>>62465347
Thanks for the bump

62465517
>>62465339
Not too bad. Way better than the last roadmap.
WHERE THE HELL IS THOTPOSTER?!?! Threads are a ghost town without thots!

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 134 replies — 35.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 134 • Updated: 2026-07-09 20:20 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62457973
>>62457562
Cope

62458144
>>62454135
They've conquered my city, not my mind. They are rude, inconsiderable, foul-smelling pricks and are insufferable to be around. They practically camp out at CostCo now. I'd rather shop at smaller, more expensive stores just to avoid their presence. There is nothing I can do about it.

62458606
>>62458144
Canadian ?

62458608
>>62458144
There is plenty you could do but you value your comfort over your country

62458622
>>62449846
LMAO

62458858
The jugaad is crazy

62460566
Jugaadjeet

62460624
>>62449441
Bloody bitch bastard

62460700
>>62449388
this is old news
I do this.
>18 months later mattress topper starts to hurt my back
>Whats the reason for the return?
>it hurts my back
>ok thank you sir, here is full refund

or the guy who returned his inflatable pool after a few months. He got the pool for his pet penguin that he ordered. after a few months and no penguin delivery he realized it was a scam and returned the pool and other things he got for the penguin.
>reason for return
>penguin order was a scam, no penguin arrived

I also know some dudes who buy wavestorms, go fuk em up, and exchange them

Costco puts the onus of returns on the manufacturer or supplier as a price for getting on the shelf in the store. just having a spot in costco is worth it enough to most companies to front all the returns for costco's lax return policy. Which is why costco DGAF and rather have customers.

62460720
>>62458144
serious question
I went bowling last week. There was a huge group of brown men. Enjoying themselves, very much, maybe too much.
They can not keep their hands off each other it was the strangest thing I've every seen.
Rubbing, patting, petting....
Each time 1 went to bowl they had to break free of all these hands and arms.
It was like a race to be the last one to touch the bowler, right up until they've thrown. like the hands and arms in drag me to hell expect brown and sensual
so bizarre.
why do they do this?
pic rel but brown and stinky

62460768
>>62455914
I for one love my country. And like true love I hate all those that threaten to jeopardize it. Enjoy your stay but you have to go back

62460799
fun fact: indians skin is brown because they each eat up to 4lbs of poop daily, like how flamingos turn pink from their diet of shrimp!

62460937
>>62460799
Oh I thought flamingos turned pink because they ate pussy

62461696
saaaaaaaaaaaars

62462254
>>62460799
wtf

62462262
>>62449388
All these office chairs are basically painted fabric. The paint flakes off after a while. They shouldn't be able to sell this junk in the first place.

62462271
>>62449388
>People
americans are not people.

62462287
>>62449441
Why do Indians talk so weird? It's like you can immediately tell they're jeets just from text alone

62462621
>>62462271
Seethe

62462950
>>62449388
I've seen people abuse their return program, and get their membership canceled.

62462960
>>62453201
i would cover her face with semen if you catch my drift

62463582
>>62449441
BENCHOOOOOD!!!!!!! WHY YOU REDEEM!! NO NO DO NOT CLICK ON THAT!!!! NOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!

62463635
>>62458606
cucks here won't do shit, they bitch and complain while patronizing Indian establishments. They will talk about the take over and then also about their favorite totally awesome pajeet they met at signhortons. The most opposition you will get is from white canadians. People will whine about the state of tim hortons while simultaneously pretending a coffee from there is a mandatory purchase they will die without

Sad, but they will have to watch canada just disintegrate with no option to fix it before they maybe realize. This whole country is full of just chicken shit low IQ cattle. Maybe 4% of canadians realize how bad this actually is and are doing the bare minimum like not hiring and giving money to the invaders, the rest are fully, entirely complicit in it. If it wasn't for how big this place was, that you can find thousands of acres with not a single person within 5km of you, I would have already left.

62464135
>>62463582
Saaars

62464324
>>62463635
Canada is just India now accept it

62464711
>>62462960
Kek

62464984
>>62449441
kek'd at the replies

62464997
>>62449441
I fucking hate Indians so much man

62465006
>>62449846
lol. Source?

62465010
>>62449441
easy (you)s

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 79 replies — 24.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-07-09 20:53 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62462723
>>62462709
Bitcoin has become more than just a payments network. That's not the same as losing its reason to exist.

62462739
>>62462709
If Bitcoin has "no reason to exist," why are people still building new infrastructure around it?

62462938
>>62462717
Because it's not just the tech anymore. Aegis and Utila getting involved tells me there's real interest in native BTC.

62462943
>>62462739
>building new infrastructure around it?
I think that's called Ethereum

62462946
>>62462943
not bitcoin?

62462953
>>62462943
ethereum is the king of it

62462965
>>62462946
Nothing is being built around or on top of Bitcoin. When people hear tokenized assets or stablecoins are being built on crypto they think crypto = Bitcoin, but everyone who understands even a bit about crypto knows this is not true. If someone disagrees, feel free to shill whatever Bitcoin has achieved in the past 1 year, or in the past 5 years for that matter. I can't think of anything besides inscriptions, which had some potential but ended up as nothing more than speculative meme-NFTs. Bitcoin isn't designed to keep up with fast moving crypto tech, it's designed to be solid and ossified. It's biggest achievement is having not changed at all, monetarily or technologically.

62463137
>>62462965
You're only looking at the base layer. A lot of the innovation is happening around Bitcoin, not by changing Bitcoin itself.

62463151
>>62463137
Such as? Besides Lightning, which is mostly just custodial wallets, there is not a single Bitcoin L2 that's used.
>but bro they are in development muh 2 weeks away
This has been the case for 5+ years now.

62463201
>>62463151
Stacks, Lombard and BoB

62463256
>>62463151
>>62463201
Babylon is worth looking into if you think nothing's being built around bitcoin.

62463258
What's the best thing to do? Buy or sell

62463262
>>62450728

62463263
>>62463201
WBTC TVL is 10x more than all of these combined. There's way more BTC lending happening on Ethereum than on BTC L2s.

62463265
>>62450728
ETF’s = whales

62463279
>>62459225
You are forgetting every nations currency have the strength of wet noodles now. They will print again soon.

62463285
bitcoin is a commodity backed ETF without the commodity

all it ever is and all it ever will be

62463301
There should be a 90s themed live action series called something cheesy like "Good morning Mr. Nakamoto" about the bitcoin creator. After making it big in his mid 20s he has become a multi billionaire, but is extremely paranoid so he decided to keep a low profile. Now he's in his late 30s, a family man with a couple kids, and the series follow an episodic format where he desperately tries to hide from his family his enormous wealth and just pretends to be an office worker. The thing is he has zero credentials so he can't actually get a job so it becomes incrementally difficult (due to plot inducement stupidity) to maintain the farce. Prying yet endearing secondary characters keep coming and leaving, and oftentimes misunderstandings make him almost blow his cover. During some weaker seasons he's stalked by some group of nerds that are almost sure he's the real deal and want to take revenge due to prices plunging, but eventually some shenanigan makes the nerds pin their suspicions on Nakamoto's rival (who was trying to cuck him with his wife, who by the way goes through a very annoying arc because she realizes his hubby is not being open with her or something). Nakamoto's personality is a bit like Hal from Malcolm in the middle but less lolrandom, he just plays as a fool to better hide his secret.

62463320
>>62463301
Some thematic episodes could include his wife hiring a financial advisor because he played the broke ass bit too much, and he tries to stop the advisor from peeking at his 100b net worth at any cost. He also gets in trouble because the advisor is not very bright and tells terrible advice very overconfidently, so Nakamoto counters with suspiciously good and sharp analysis (fortunately his wife just doesnt care because he has ingrained the dumb with money narrative and the advisor is very confident, and also a financial advisor duh). In another episode he gets pinned by the IRS after accidentally doing some billion level transaction and having to pay tenths of millions in taxes. Another one where he (again, Plot) is revealed to have all the money behind a 20 word seed, but he doesnt remember the words, so it's a musical where he finally manages to recall them through a catchy song. The episode is rated quite poorly though because it doesn't fit with the general tone. In the seventh and last season his younger kid finds out by dumb luck and the rest of that season is him preventing them from telling mom. The show gets cancelled afterwards due do decline in viewership and the plot never gets finished.

62463429
>>62463301
>>62463320
This has potential

62464033
>>62463263
but it doesn't mean it'll stay that way.

62464098
>>62463429
I could foresee an actual ending where he has to let all his earnings go because of some contrived dumb reason, but it's framed in a way that looks somewhat deep and poignant. His cover is blown in the process, but as he's revealed to be the creator of Bitcoin, he suddently gets a call from one of the companies he tried to apply the most though all the seasons (let's say it's a mid-sized company that offers an average salary, it has it's wacky employees and boss, they usually appear for like 10 minutes every few episodes just to reject Nakamoto). He's finally accepted and he merrily joins (it's a subtle joke because now that he's a public figure he could easily be gunning for 500k jobs, but he settles for this as a nudge to the audience), managing to achieve the life he pretended to have (and actually wanted) from the get go. The series end with him walking into the office's slightly rundown and shabby building with a bright smile. You even get to make that cheap overused allegory of ''choosing happiness and being true to your principles over riches''. I feel like there's an alternate universe where this would have killed it.

62464115
>>62463256
I'll keep testing TBVs on Aave V4. BTC has a lot more going on now than just being a store of value.

62464121
>>62463301
The best ending would be nobody ever finding out if it's really him. Keeping the mystery alive is probably the most Satoshi thing possible.

62464142
>>62464098
You're too invested in that drama. It's not happening in real life.

62464251
>>62464121
You know what? That's fair. I relied too much on 90s sitcom tropes. It should just end episodically, there's just no more seasons and that's it.
>>62464142
I don't have much going on in my life and my family and friends find my occurrences boring. And it's supposed to be a comedy. What drama? I want it to be goofy and for all publics!

62464760
>>62464033
Take stablecoins for another example. Zero. Zilch. Nada. $0.00 -- Remember when 100% of USDT used to be on Bitcoin? Now it's 0%, Omni is dead and Tether has moved to every other chain. There is nothing more to Bitcoin than BTC the asset.

62464820
I have a credit card that gives me cash back in the form of BTC. So my investment strategy is just skimming off my purchases anyway.

62465005
>>62464251
Why do you think you're right about that and you think you're so sure about it?

62465089
>>62465005
I don't understand the question. Right about what.
